title: Luo Sisters Knowledge Base
summary: The first long archive is split into a navigable, generated, auditable knowledge layer. Showcase pages stay visual; knowledge pages carry the content depth; the old archive remains the source record.

intro_cards.title: Showcase And Knowledge Separation
intro_cards.text: The index and character sheets stay lightweight, attractive, and responsive. Knowledge pages carry character, story, prompt, workflow, and asset-planning depth. The old archive stays intact as the source record.

intro_cards.title: Migration Is Not Verbatim Copying
intro_cards.text: The first pass builds full navigation, summaries, and source anchors. Later passes can expand individual pages without losing the original archive.

intro_cards.title: Prevent False Content Loss
intro_cards.text: Each knowledge page shows structured content, migration status, and old archive anchors. If something looks missing, check knowledge pages, then the old archive, then docs/content_map.md.

pages.title: Navigation And Reading Paths
pages.summary: The old project navigation page is promoted into a structured knowledge-base entry that explains how showcase pages, knowledge pages, archive anchors, and production docs connect.
pages.structured_content: Showcase entry, knowledge reading paths, old archive anchor map, production documentation entry

sections.title: New Entry Responsibilities
sections.summary: The new site can jump across pages and split content, but every entry must have a clear job.

items.title: Showcase Entry
items.text: Use the index and character sheets to inspect visual presentation, character sheets, and asset slots.

items.title: Knowledge Entry
items.text: Use the knowledge index for navigation, characters, story, visual prompts, production rules, and asset planning.

items.title: Archive Entry
items.text: Use the old HTML archive as the full source record. Open source anchors only when original wording is needed.

items.title: Production Entry
items.text: Asset generation, parallel ownership, issue memory, and asset registry live in workflows, tools, and logs.

sections.title: Choose Entry By Task
sections.summary: Agents should not read the whole site at once. Enter by task, then follow the next link.
rows: The index is visual; the navigation page is structural.
Use old #characters only for source verification.
Story flow and AIGC rhythm live on the story page.
Prompt code blocks live on the visual page.
Asset list and next steps live on the workflow page.
Summarize with read_html_doc.py before opening raw HTML.

pages.title: Characters And Persona Layer
pages.summary: The sisters' eight-layer personas, relationship formula, interaction examples, and reusable character memory points.
pages.structured_content: Eight-layer personas, sister relationship formula, relationship progression, interaction example

sections.title: Character Positioning
sections.summary: Make the characters work as people first, then let the theme grow out of their choices.

items.title: Luo Qingyou
items.text: Planner, recorder, reality anchor, Chinese-inspired classic Lolita. She protects dreams through order.

items.title: Luo Arisu
items.text: Imagination, doorway, Alice motif, JK Lolita. She searches for dreams through disorder.

items.title: Relationship Formula
items.text: Arisu gives Qingyou a dream; Qingyou gives Arisu a path. One supplies arrival, the other supplies the reason to depart.

sections.title: Eight-Layer Personas
sections.summary: Expandable details preserve the depth of the old archive while keeping the page easy to scan.

groups.title: Luo Qingyou | Protecting Dreams Through Order
groups.intro: Her anchors are sage classic Lolita styling, project journals, cameras, tea, old streets, cloud collars, frog buttons, and celadon green.

items.title: 1. First Impression
items.text: Sage Chinese-inspired classic Lolita, scholar-like mood, cloud collar, frog buttons, lace, old camera, journal, tea snacks. Her first impression is gentle reliability.

items.title: 2. Daily Layer
items.text: Arrives early, carries clips, bandages, tape, sticky notes, and scissors. Checks the light before people when taking photos.

items.title: 3. Behavioral Memory
items.text: When Arisu proposes an idea, Qingyou opens her journal before answering. When tense, she adjusts cuffs or camera straps and leaves an 'unexpected' column in plans.

items.title: 4. Ability Layer
items.text: Planning, coordination, aesthetic judgment, archival work, photography, execution, and risk planning.

items.title: 5. Relationship Layer
items.text: Arisu's imagination is the brightest surprise in Qingyou's life. Qingyou protects and prepares, but must learn to leave room for Arisu's dream to breathe.

items.title: 6. Anxiety Response
items.text: Qingyou uses order against anxiety. Her kindness carries fatigue because she often turns care into responsibility.

items.title: 7. Hidden Conflict
items.text: AIGC shakes her value: if tools can plan, organize, and optimize, what remains of the skills she relied on?

items.title: 8. Growth Layer
items.text: She moves from doing every step by hand to choosing what must stay human: judgment, relationships, aesthetics, and shared experience.

groups.title: Luo Arisu | Searching For Dreams Through Disorder
groups.intro: Her anchors are blue-white JK Lolita styling, keys, rabbits, pocket watches, old libraries, secret doors, daisies, and Alice motifs.

items.title: 1. First Impression
items.text: Blue-white JK Lolita, blouse, pinafore, bow, daisy, key necklace, rabbit charm, pocket-watch bag, and old books. She feels like an Alice figure searching for a rabbit hole at school.

items.title: 2. Daily Layer
items.text: Stops at hallway doors, gives names to charms and objects, and writes diary entries like story openings.

items.title: 3. Behavioral Memory
items.text: Touches her key necklace while thinking and her bow when nervous. She notices doors, mirrors, stairs, and windows longer than other people do.

items.title: 4. Ability Layer
items.text: Concept creation, mood naming, fairy-tale expression, and noticing strange feelings inside ordinary life.

items.title: 5. Relationship Layer
items.text: She depends on Qingyou's execution, but needs her odd ideas to be preserved rather than normalized.

items.title: 6. Anxiety Response
items.text: Arisu uses exploration against anxiety. Detours, daydreaming, and strange sentences delay pressure until she can face it.

items.title: 7. Hidden Conflict
items.text: She fears reality will not match imagination. Perfect generated Wonderlands attract her because they keep dreams beautiful and risk-free.

items.title: 8. Growth Layer
items.text: She moves from waiting for the white rabbit to choosing the door herself, keeping wonder while accepting consequences.

sections.title: Relationship Progression
sections.summary: Turn complementarity, conflict, and repair into scenes that can be written or illustrated.

items.title: Progression
items.text: Early: light comedic complementarity. Middle: cute habits become pressure. Late: one learns to leave blanks; the other learns to choose.

items.title: Interaction Example
items.text: Arisu proposes a tea party only lost people can attend. Qingyou answers by giving the dream a date. Their conflict is how to plan getting lost without closing the door.

pages.title: Story Outline And Theme Layer
pages.summary: Rabbit Hole Tea Party plot, chapter progression, AIGC rhythm, supporting character functions, and key scene hooks.
pages.structured_content: Three story lines, chapter table, AIGC rhythm, supporting cast functions, scene hooks

sections.title: Three Story Lines
sections.summary: The surface is the tea party, the middle is the sister relationship, and the deeper layer is anxiety and choice in the AIGC era.

items.title: Surface Line
items.text: The sisters prepare a small Alice-flavored tea party: venue, outfits, invitations, photos, sweets, entry route, and decorations.

items.title: Emotional Line
items.text: Qingyou wants the dream to land safely; Arisu wants the door to keep glowing.

items.title: Era Line
items.text: AIGC gradually appears as tool, companion, adviser, and recommender. It helps the characters and amplifies their coping habits.

sections.title: Chapter Progression
sections.summary: Preserve public events, character changes, and AIGC exposure in a compact expansion table.
rows: Arisu proposes the lost-person tea party; Qingyou starts recording.
AI poster generation appears as casual school life.
They search for the old library, draft invitations, and design blue-white and sage-green outfits.
The White Rabbit system offers reference images and route suggestions.
Qingyou's handmade draft is easily outperformed by an AI version.
Qingyou uses an LLM to optimize the invitation and flattens Arisu's fairy-tale sentence.
Arisu feels her door has closed; Qingyou feels misunderstood.
The tea party becomes more perfect and more empty; Arisu sinks into generated Wonderland.
Qingyou is trapped in task lists; Arisu is trapped in a perfect dream.
Generation, companionship, and advice all surface.
They begin writing prompts and boundaries together.
AI returns to an assistant role.
A rainy tea party begins and the accidental parts become the memory.

sections.title: AIGC Appearance Rhythm
sections.summary: AIGC should not steal the visible story; it becomes an anxiety amplifier in the middle and late phases.

items.title: Early | 5%
items.text: Background air: posters, references, schedules.

items.title: Early-Mid | 15%
items.text: Efficiency contrast: Qingyou feels a small sting.

items.title: Middle | 35%
items.text: Side characters show companion, adviser, and generator uses.

items.title: Mid-Late | 45%
items.text: AIGC directly amplifies the sisters' conflict.

items.title: Late | 20%
items.text: The tool returns to the background while real experience closes the theme.

sections.title: Supporting Cast And AI Use Styles
sections.summary: Side characters carry companionship, advice, and system-entry dimensions.

items.title: Nanxing | AI As Companion
items.text: A long-term chat companion gives stable memory and comfort. Her growth is toward slight risk in real relationships.

items.title: Mingxia | AI As Adviser
items.text: A student-council type who asks systems to rate risk, traffic, and routes. She represents the temptation of optimal answers.

items.title: White Rabbit System | Era Entry
items.text: A bundle of recommendation, generation, reminders, and guidance. Useful, cheerful, and always a little too urgent.

sections.title: Key Scene Hooks
sections.summary: Reusable memory points for chapters, key visuals, and future web displays.
items: Arisu writes an invitation asking guests to bring one piece of getting lost before the tea cools.
An LLM makes the invitation clearer and more formal; Arisu says it closed her door.
Arisu tells Qingyou that she even scheduled getting lost.
Qingyou admits she is afraid of becoming unnecessary if everything she is good at can be replaced.
The sisters rewrite prompts together: preserve the feeling of getting lost while ensuring guests can return safely.
Rain, puddles, umbrellas, wrong turns, and laughter become the real memory.

pages.title: Visual Direction And Prompt Library
pages.summary: Art direction, post-layout text rules, expression rules, duo key visual, and prompt groups for both sisters.
pages.structured_content: Art direction, prompt groups, expression rules, duo key visual

sections.title: Unified Art Direction
sections.summary: Images serve character identity and reproducibility first; final text belongs to web or post-layout tools.

items.title: Style
items.text: Japanese anime character sheet, clean lineart, soft cel shading, clear detail, low-saturation colors, light-novel cover and production-reference mood.

items.title: Image Control
items.text: Readable outfit construction, reusable accessories, stable hair silhouettes, and clear color roles.

items.title: Text Rule
items.text: Generate characters, outfits, ornaments, and blank layout space first. Add titles, labels, and long copy through HTML, SVG, or PIL.

items.title: Expression Rule
items.text: Every expression must visibly change brows, eyes, mouth, head angle, gesture, or shoulders.

sections.title: General Prompt Base
sections.summary: Prompt content uses code blocks for copying, versioning, and later refinement.

items.title: General Base Prompt
items.code: Japanese anime character design sheet, clean precise lineart, soft cel shading, elegant pastel color palette, high detail outfit construction, readable accessories, full body, balanced proportions, expressive eyes, gentle light, character reference sheet style, white or warm neutral background, clear silhouette, organized layout, production-ready design

pages.title: Production Rules And Asset Planning
pages.summary: Japanese anime character-project production flow, asset list, unified checklist, and next-step suggestions.
pages.structured_content: Production flow, asset list, unified checklist, next steps

sections.title: Anime Character Project Production Flow
sections.summary: Move through concept, line exploration, standing art, reference sheet, key art, and application assets.
rows: Name, signature line, theme role, relationships, fixed identity marks.
One sentence explains who she is; three memory points stay stable.
Outfit, hair, palette, material, props, scene references.
Qingyou leans sage-classic-tea; Arisu leans blue-school-Alice.
They remain distinct when detail is hidden.
Hair, skirt, collar, cuffs, socks, shoes, bags.
Outfit layers and accessory positions are clear.
Main, secondary, and accent colors have clear jobs.
Usable as the parent image for later work.
Front, side, back, with key structure notes.
Skirt, hair ornaments, straps, socks, shoes, and back bow are complete.
Brows, eyes, mouth, head angle, and gesture visibly differ.
Standing, sitting, photographing, journaling, door-looking, invitation-giving.
Actions serve character, not just beauty.
Standard, casual, tea-party, rainy-day, winter, light campus outfits.
Core identity marks survive the outfit change.
Camera, journal, key, pocket-watch bag, invitation, tea cup, school bag.
Each prop can tell story on its own.
Two solo key arts each and one or two duo key visuals.
Key art expresses theme, relationship, and growth.
Avatars, chibi art, stickers, banners, Live2D rough splits.

items.title: Luo Arisu Asset List
items.text: Standard standing art, turnaround, curious/surprised/dreamy/shy/hurt/brave expressions, door-looking/key-holding/invitation-writing/charm-swinging/threshold poses, key necklace, pocket-watch bag, rabbit charm, old book, music box, blue ink, corridor door, before-tea-cools scene.

sections.title: Unified Checklist
sections.summary: Reuse these rules during generation, filing, and web linking.
items: When Qingyou changes outfits, preserve sage green, journal or camera, and Chinese-inspired structure cues.
When Arisu changes outfits, preserve blue-white, key or rabbit, and campus/Alice cues.
Every image should answer: does this make the character clearer?
Every prop needs narrative function: relationship, personality, or theme.
Prefer key art that captures an event over pure standing display.
If a crop is skewed, tight, or unclear, consult the full source_sheet before editing crop_manifest.

sections.title: Next-Step Suggestions
sections.summary: Standardize references before generation and manual correction.

items.title: 1. Fix Character Cards
items.text: Confirm age, height, birthday, school or club, family relation, speech style, and common props.

items.title: 2. Make First Turnarounds
items.text: Start with line art or low-color roughs, inspect silhouette and structure, then add material detail.

items.title: 3. Choose First Duo Key Visual
items.text: Start with Door And Journal: Arisu finds the door, Qingyou records and prepares the path back.
